firstTimeBuyerRebate.eligibilitySummary: Must be a Canadian citizen or permanent resident, 18+ years old, never owned a home anywhere in the world, and must occupy the home as principal residence within 9 months of purchase.

nonResidentSpeculationTax.description: Non-Resident Speculation Tax (NRST) applies to foreign nationals and foreign corporations purchasing residential property in Ontario.

firstTimeBuyerRebate.eligibilitySummary: Must qualify for the provincial first-time buyer rebate. The municipal rebate mirrors provincial eligibility criteria.

firstTimeBuyerExemption.eligibilitySummary: Must be a Canadian citizen or permanent resident, have lived in BC for 12 consecutive months prior to registration or filed 2 income tax returns in BC in the last 6 years, never owned a principal residence anywhere, and occupy the property as principal residence for the first year.

newlyBuiltHomeExemption.eligibilitySummary: Applies to newly built homes. Buyer must be a Canadian citizen or permanent resident and occupy as principal residence.

foreignBuyerTax.description: Additional Property Transfer Tax for foreign nationals in designated areas.
foreignBuyerTax.banNote: Foreign buyer ban is in effect until January 2027 under the Prohibition on the Purchase of Residential Property by Non-Canadians Act.

provincial.note: The Welcome Tax is set and collected by municipalities. The general bracket structure is set by provincial law; Montreal applies higher rates under its own by-law.

AB.name: Alberta
AB.note: Alberta does not charge a land transfer tax. Instead, the Land Titles Office charges flat registration fees for the title transfer and mortgage registration.

titleTransfer.description: Title transfer fee: $50 base + $5 per $5,000 of property value (or part thereof)

mortgageRegistration.description: Mortgage registration fee: $50 base + $5 per $5,000 of mortgage amount (or part thereof)

SK.name: Saskatchewan
SK.note: Saskatchewan does not charge a land transfer tax. Instead, Information Services Corporation (ISC) charges a title transfer fee based on the property value.

titleTransfer.description: Title transfer fee: 0.3% of property value

NS.name: Nova Scotia
NS.note: Nova Scotia's Deed Transfer Tax is set and collected by each municipality. Rates typically range from 0.5% to 1.5%.

provincial.note: No provincial deed transfer tax. Tax is municipal only.

municipalities.name: Other
municipalities.note: Default estimate — verify with local municipality

NL.name: Newfoundland and Labrador
NL.note: Newfoundland and Labrador charges a land registration fee rather than a land transfer tax.

titleTransfer.description: $100 flat fee + $0.40 per $100 of property value over $500

firstTimeBuyerRebate.eligibilitySummary: First-time home buyers may be eligible for a full rebate of the Real Property Transfer Tax. Must be a Canadian citizen or permanent resident, have been a resident of PEI for at least 12 months, and the property must be used as a principal residence. Property value limits may apply — verify with PEI Finance.

YT.name: Yukon
YT.note: Yukon charges no land transfer tax. A small land title registration fee applies.

titleTransfer.description: Small registration fee applies. Contact Yukon Land Titles Office for current fee schedule.

NT.name: Northwest Territories
NT.note: Northwest Territories charges no land transfer tax. A small land title registration fee applies.

titleTransfer.description: Small registration fee applies. Contact NWT Land Titles Office for current fee schedule.

NU.name: Nunavut
NU.note: Nunavut charges no land transfer tax. A small land title registration fee applies.

titleTransfer.description: Small registration fee applies. Contact Nunavut Land Titles Office for current fee schedule.
